title: EDI@Energy AHB Profile
description: Application Handbook (AHB) rules per Pruefidentifikator for one (message_type, release) pair.

schema_version.description: Schema version number. Current maximum supported version is 1. Versions higher than MAX_SCHEMA_VERSION in xtask/src/codegen.rs are rejected. Additive-only changes (new optional fields with defaults) do not require a version bump.

valid_until.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) after which this profile expires. Absent means open-ended.

ahb_revision.description: Human-readable AHB revision identifier, e.g. 'AHB S2.1, Stand 01.10.2024'.

source_document.description: Identifies the BDEW PDF used to author this profile, e.g. 'UTILMD AHB S2.1 vom 01.10.2024'.

supersedes_directory.description: Folder name of the prior release that this profile supersedes, e.g. 'fv20241001'.

reviewed_by.description: Name or identifier of the person who reviewed this profile before production use.

reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this profile was reviewed and promoted to production.

Requirement.description: M = mandatory (Muss), C = conditional (BDEW Bedingungsoperator — requires conditional_rules), S = should (Soll — warning if absent), O = optional (Kann), N = not used (must not appear).

ConditionalRule.description: A BDEW Bedingungsoperator rule. Supported operators: I (if present → mandatory), V (if absent → mandatory), E (if present → forbidden), X (exclusive — exactly one of this or secondary_tag), U (at least one of this or secondary_tag), O (at most one of this or secondary_tag), G (repeat count gate), Z (qualifier-gated I/E).

operator.description: BDEW Bedingungsoperator code. Defaults to I (if trigger present → mandatory).

secondary_tag.description: Second segment tag for two-operand operators (X, U, O). Optional for K (at most one); use additional_tags for K rules with ≥3 operands.

count_threshold.description: Repeat-count threshold for the G operator: the condition fires when the trigger segment occurs ≥ count_threshold times.

when_tag.description: The segment tag to look for as the condition trigger (e.g. 'QTY').

when_element_index.description: 0-based element index in the trigger segment. Default 0.

when_value.description: If present, the condition fires only when the trigger segment has this value at when_element_index. If absent, any occurrence of when_tag triggers the condition.

when_value_alternatives.description: Alternative values for when_value (OR semantics). The condition fires when any of these values appears at when_element_index.

when_additional_elements.description: Additional element conditions that must ALL hold simultaneously (AND semantics) with the primary when_tag/when_value condition.

element_index.description: 0-based element index in the trigger segment.

then_requirement.description: M = this segment becomes mandatory when the condition holds; N = this segment must not appear when the condition holds; S = this segment should appear (Soll) when the condition holds — absence produces a Warning, not an Error.

then_qualifier_index.description: 0-based element index to check in the TARGET segment when then_requirement='M'. When combined with then_qualifier_value, the mandatory check requires that at least one occurrence of the target segment has this value at the given element index. Default 0.

then_qualifier_value.description: Required element value in the target segment (at then_qualifier_index). If present, the mandatory check asserts that at least one occurrence of target tag has this qualifier value — not merely that the tag appears at all.

_description.description: Human-readable BDEW condition reference, e.g. '[92] Wenn QTY DE6063 mit Wert 67 vorhanden'.

additional_tags.description: Extra segment tags (beyond when_tag and secondary_tag) for the K operator. Together they form the complete set of mutually-exclusive tags.

allowed_values.description: Exhaustive list of allowed values for this element, if restricted.

element_index.description: 0-based element index within the segment. Must be set explicitly — there is no default to avoid silently checking element 0 when the DE is at a different position (F-021).

required_qualifiers.description: At least one occurrence of this segment must have the specified qualifier value for the given DE.

conditional_rules.description: BDEW Bedingungsoperator rules. Required when requirement='C'. Each rule specifies a trigger condition (presence/value of another segment) and the effective requirement for this segment when the condition holds. Multiple rules are independent (OR semantics — any trigger fires the consequence independently).

_description.description: Human-readable note documenting the rule origin, referenced BDEW condition number, or context. Not used in code generation.

group_rules.description: Per-segment-group-instance rules (F-001). Each entry is evaluated once per occurrence of the named group rather than across the flat message segment list.

GroupRule.description: An AHB rule scoped to a specific segment-group instance. Unlike segment_rules (which scan the flat message), group_rules fire once per occurrence of group_id, checking only the segments within that group window. Use conditional_rules to express BDEW Bedingungsoperator conditions that must hold within a single group occurrence (F-001 fix).

group_id.description: Segment-group identifier as defined in the MIG profile (e.g. "SG4").

tag.description: EDIFACT segment tag to check within each group instance (e.g. "STS").

requirement.description: M = mandatory in every group occurrence, N = must not appear in any occurrence, O = optional (qualifier_restrictions still enforced when present), C = conditional (use conditional_rules to specify the conditions).

qualifier_restrictions.description: Qualifier restrictions applied per group instance. Maps element DE identifier to list of allowed values.

conditional_rules.description: BDEW Bedingungsoperator rules scoped to each group occurrence. Each rule is evaluated against only the segments within the current group instance (segs parameter in with_scoped_group_rule_fn). This resolves F-001: intra-SG conditional dependencies are now evaluated per-instance rather than against the flat message. Required when requirement='C'. Multiple rules are independent (OR semantics — any trigger fires independently).

_description.description: Human-readable note documenting the AHB rule source.
